sections.paragraph:
m. (comp. of אֲרִי and אֵל).

(1) lion of God, i.e. very mighty hero. Collect. 2Sa 23:20, שְׁנֵי אֲרִיאֵל מֹואָב “two heroes of Moab;” see אַרְאֵלּ and אֲרִי 1Ch 11:22. (Compare Arab. اُللَّهِ أَسَدُ and اُللَّهِ لَيْتُ lion of God, an epithet of brave men, and Pers. شير خدا Shiri khoda, lion of God.) Isa 29:1, Isa 29:2, used of Jerusalem as the “city of heroes,” which is to be unconquered; although others, comparing the passage of Ezekiel about to be cited, render it hearth, i.e. altar of God.

(2) hearth of God (compare إِرَةُ hearth, chimney, from the root אָרָה No. II.), used of the altar of burnt-offering, Eze 43:15, 16 Eze 43:16.

(3) [Ariel], pr.n. of a man, Ezr 8:16.
